ground_truth.title: Where to Find Affordable Dental Care in Charlotte, NC - C.W. Williams Community Health Center, Inc.
ground_truth.author: Adeyemi Niniola
ground_truth.main_content: Maintaining good oral health is crucial, but the cost of dental care is often a barrier for many people. If you're in Charlotte, NC, and are looking for affordable dental care, you're in luck. There are several options available that ensure you can maintain your oral health without breaking the bank. This guide will point you in the right direction for primary and preventive care. One of the most accessible and affordable ways to get dental care is through local community health centers. These centers offer a range of dental services, including checkups, cleanings, fillings, and extractions, at a fraction of the cost of private practices. At C.W. Williams Community Health Center (CWWCHC), we provide high-quality dental services with a focus on accessibility. Our services are designed to meet the needs of families and individuals from all backgrounds, and we offer sliding-scale fees based on income, ensuring everyone has access to the care they need. Federally Qualified Health Centers (FQHCs) are another option for affordable dental care in Charlotte. These centers receive government funding to provide services to underserved populations, which allows them to offer lower prices. FQHCs often provide a wide range of dental services, including preventive care, restorative treatments, and emergency dental care. If you're eligible for Medicaid or the Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP), you can access dental services at little to no cost. These government programs cover a variety of dental treatments including checkups, cleanings, and even some more complex procedures. If you require more expensive dental procedures, such as crowns or orthodontics, payment plans and financing options can help you manage the cost. Many dental offices, including CWWCHC, offer flexible payment options to ensure you can access the care you need without financial strain. If you don't have dental insurance, discount dental plans can be a great way to save on routine visits and procedures. At CWWCHC, we offer a sliding scale fee based on income and family size. At CWWCHC, we understand that dental care is key to your overall health, and we aim to make it as affordable and accessible as possible. Our team is equipped to treat a wide range of dental problems, including stained teeth, toothache, enamel erosion, tooth decay, bad breath, cavities, tooth and gum sensitivity, and gum disease.
